title: Inflammation. Effects of Grape Consumption on Biomarkers of Inflammation, Endothelial Function, and Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cell Gene Expression in Obese Subjects. This study investigated the effects of grape consumption on biomarkers of cardiovascular health in obese participants in both postprandial and chronic settings. Twenty obese adults consumed 60 grams whole grape powder or placebo followed by high fat high carbohydrate meal challenge. Following acute challenge, participants consumed their respective treatment regimen daily for 4 weeks to determine effects of chronic consumption. Acute grape consumption resulted in decreased vasoconstrictor E T-1 concentration and increased gene expression related to oxidative stress defense following a high-fat meal. Four weeks of daily grape consumption had little effect on cardiovascular biomarkers or gene expression measured in this study, although some increase in s V C A M-1 concentration was observed.

sentences.text: Inflammation.
Effects of Grape Consumption on Biomarkers of Inflammation, Endothelial Function, and Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cell Gene Expression in Obese Subjects.
This study investigated the effects of grape consumption on biomarkers of cardiovascular health in obese participants in both postprandial and chronic settings.
Twenty obese adults consumed 60 grams whole grape powder or placebo followed by high fat high carbohydrate meal challenge.
Following acute challenge, participants consumed their respective treatment regimen daily for 4 weeks to determine effects of chronic consumption.
Acute grape consumption resulted in decreased vasoconstrictor E T-1 concentration and increased gene expression related to oxidative stress defense following a high-fat meal.
Four weeks of daily grape consumption had little effect on cardiovascular biomarkers or gene expression measured in this study, although some increase in s V C A M-1 concentration was observed.

Dietary Grape Powder Increases Interleukin-1 Beta and Interleukin-6 Production by Lipopolysaccharide-Activated Monocytes and Reduces Plasma Concentrations of Large L D L and Large L D L-Cholesterol Particles in Obese Humans.

This randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, cross-over study with freeze-dried grape powder in obese men and women found that those consuming grape powder had increased production of I L-1 beta and I L-6 supernatants from lipopolysaccharide-activated peripheral blood mononuclear cells compared with the placebo powder group.
These results suggest an increase in the sensitivity of monocytes in a population at greater risk of developing infections.
No difference in production of T-cell cytokines was observed.
Additionally, consuming grapes reduced the plasma concentrations of atherogenic lipid fractions, large L D L-cholesterol and large L D L particles, compared with the placebo group.
Grape Consumption's Effects on Fitness, Muscle Injury, Mood, and Perceived Health.
In this study, 40 recreationally active young adults consumed either a grape or placebo drink for 45 consecutive days.
Before and after measures of treadmill-running V O 2 max, mood, and perceived health status were made.

High intensity eccentric actions of nondominant elbow flexors were also performed, and arm muscle inflammation, pain, and function were measured before and two days after the elbow exercises were done.
No significant effect of grape consumption was found on any of the outcomes with this group of volunteers.
Effects of Consumption of Whole Grape Powder on Basal N F-Kappa B Signaling and Inflammatory Cytokine Secretion in a Mouse Model of Inflammation.
In this study of inflammation in an animal model, consuming a 4 percent grape-enriched diet significantly reduced baseline levels of inflammatory signaling, N F-kappa B activity.

Although no significant benefit was seen against externally induced sources of inflammation in both acute and repeated exposures, the baseline levels of inflammatory cytokines, I L-6 and T N F-alpha, in the animals within the acute challenge group were lower in the grape-fed group versus the control group.
The investigators note that persistent low-grade inflammation is a hallmark of many chronic diseases and that a grape-enriched diet may help suppress this harmful effect.
Grape Powder Extract Attenuates Tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha-Mediated Inflammation and Insulin Resistance in Primary Cultures of Human Adipocytes.
This study examined the effects of grapes on markers of inflammation and insulin resistance in primary cultures of newly differentiated human adipocytes treated with T N F-alpha.

Results showed that grape extract reduced the expression of a number of inflammatory genes, including I L-6, I L-1 beta, C O X-2, and M C P-1, and prevented or reduced the expression of negative regulators of insulin sensitivity and suppressors of insulin-stimulated glucose uptake.
Potential Mechanisms by Which Polyphenol-Rich Grapes Prevent Obesity-Mediated Inflammation and Metabolic Diseases.
This review article highlights the ways in which grapes and grape polyphenols have been reported to reduce inflammation, thus exhibiting potential to prevent metabolic diseases.

Five key mechanisms were emphasized: acting as an antioxidant or promoting antioxidant activity; attenuating endoplasmic reticulum stress signaling; blocking proinflammatory proteins and transcription factors involved in metabolic disease; suppressing inflammatory gene expression; or activating transcription factors that may reduce obesity-mediated chronic inflammation.
Polyphenol-Rich Grape Powder Extract Attenuates Inflammation in Human Macrophages and in Human Adipocytes Exposed to Macrophage-Conditioned Media.
In a cell study of immune and fat cells, grapes were shown to reduce inflammation and insulin resistance, thus improving glucose uptake by fat cells and helping to protect important cell functions.
Obesity and type 2 diabetes are both linked to chronic inflammation in fat tissue.
